events.description: Senator Murray introduced S. 716 to authorize issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters for land on Crow Indian Reservation
George Peters sent letter to Senator Murray requesting assistance with patent in fee
Senator Murray replied to George Peters advising he was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of patent in fee
Department of the Interior issued report on S. 716 recommending enactment with amendments, signed by Undersecretary Oscar L. Chapman
Senator O'Mahoney sent letter to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and requesting advice on amendments
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
House Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment, committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed
Senator Murray sent letter to George Peters enclosing copy of House report and advising bill passed Senate and was reported favorably from House Committee on Public Lands
S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senator Murray sent telegram to George Peters advising S. 716 passed House and cleared for President's signature
George Peters indicated desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian
S. 716 was read twice and referred to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters' land on Crow Indian Reservation to another Crow Indian under existing regulations as authorized by S. 716
